skill.name: network-monitoring
skill.description: Monitor UniFi network infrastructure including sites, devices, and system health. Diagnose connectivity issues, track device performance, and generate network diagnostics.
skill.summary: Monitor UniFi sites, device health, and network performance with structured diagnostics.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backtick text as shell execution and line 3 as weak cryptography. Review found no executable code, no cryptographic operation, no prompt injection, and no data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Lines 31-55 contain Markdown inline code for documented tool names and workflows. They are not Ruby shell backticks or executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside a Markdown documentation list and workflow steps. No shell syntax, script block, or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: Line 3 is frontmatter description text about network monitoring. No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithms or cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entire line is natural language metadata. It contains no hash names, encryption calls, imports, or security-sensitive implementation.

content.value_statement: Network teams need fast visibility into UniFi site health, device status, and system metrics. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured monitoring and diagnostic workflows.
actual_capabilities: Lists configured UniFi sites through the documented monitoring tools.
Checks site health before deeper device investigation.
Lists network devices for a selected site.
Retrieves device statistics such as CPU, memory, and uptime.
Uses system information to support network troubleshooting.
Helps produce diagnostic summaries for network issues.
limitations: Requires working UniFi monitoring tools in the host environment.
Does not include its own network scanner or collector.
Cannot repair hardware or controller configuration directly.
Results depend on the freshness of available UniFi data.

use_cases.title: Daily Site Health Review
use_cases.description: Check all UniFi sites for degraded health and identify sites that need attention.

use_cases.title: Device Performance Triage
use_cases.description: Review device status, CPU, memory, and uptime when users report slow connectivity.

use_cases.title: Multi-Site Infrastructure Report
use_cases.description: Compare health across several sites and summarize issues for stakeholders.

prompt_templates.title: Check All Sites
prompt_templates.prompt: List all configured UniFi sites and summarize the health status for each site.

prompt_templates.title: Find Offline Devices
prompt_templates.prompt: Show devices that are offline or degraded for the selected site. Include likely impact and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Device Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review device statistics for high CPU, high memory, and unusual uptime patterns. Prioritize the most urgent devices.

prompt_templates.title: Create Diagnostic Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a network diagnostic report using site health, device lists, device statistics, and system information.

output_examples.input: What is the health status of all my sites?
output_examples.output: A concise site summary with health labels, device counts, degraded areas, and recommended follow-up actions.

output_examples.input: Which devices are underperforming in the main office?
output_examples.output: A prioritized device list with status, resource pressure, uptime concerns, and suggested troubleshooting steps.

output_examples.input: Generate a network diagnostic report for slow connectivity.
output_examples.output: A structured report covering site health, affected devices, system indicators, likely causes, and escalation guidance.

best_practices: Start with site health before investigating individual devices.
Correlate device metrics with user reports and incident timing.
Document repeated patterns for capacity planning and hardware review.
anti_patterns: Do not assume one high metric explains every connectivity issue.
Do not skip site health when multiple sites may be affected.
Do not treat stale controller data as live operational evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to UniFi by itself?
faq.answer: No. It depends on available UniFi monitoring tools in the host environment.

faq.question: Can it show offline devices?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow lists devices and highlights offline or degraded status.

faq.question: Can it diagnose slow connectivity?
faq.answer: Yes. It combines site health, device statistics, and system information to guide troubleshooting.

faq.question: Does it change network settings?
faq.answer: No. The skill documentation describes monitoring and diagnostics, not configuration changes.

faq.question: Is it useful for multiple sites?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports listing sites and comparing health across infrastructure.

faq.question: What metrics can it review?
faq.answer: It can guide review of health status, device counts, CPU, memory, uptime, and system information.
